---
title: "The Cascadia Family"
subtitle: "One architecture. Three domains. Production-grade analytics from source to report."
---

Every Cascadia build runs on the same modern Microsoft data stack, end to end:

1. **Source (SQL Server).** A relational, star-schema-ready operational database — the system of record. Dimensions and facts, a date dimension, surrogate keys, loaded reproducibly from source files.
2. **Pipeline (Microsoft Fabric medallion lakehouse).** Bronze (raw) → Silver (cleaned/conformed) → Gold (modeled) layers, so the path from raw data to trustworthy tables is explicit and auditable.
3. **Semantic model & reporting (Power BI).** A governed semantic model with a dedicated measures layer and clean relationships, surfaced in focused reports designed for non-technical audiences.

The value of doing it as a family is repeatability: once the pattern is proven on one domain, standing up the next is fast and consistent. Cascadia Medical Devices proved the pattern on manufacturing; Cascadia Pharmacy applies it to real public healthcare data; Cascadia Clothing extends it to retail.

---

## Shared Architecture

Every Cascadia domain runs the same end-to-end stack:

**SQL Server (local)** → **Microsoft Fabric Gateway** → **Fabric Lakehouse (Bronze / Silver / Gold)** → **Power BI Semantic Model** → **Power BI Report**

::: {.arch-container}
![Cascadia end-to-end data platform](assets/cascadia_analytics_tech_stack.svg)
:::

---

## The Three Domains

| Domain | Status | Data | Headline Skill |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Cascadia Medical Devices](projects/cascadia-medical-devices.qmd) | Live | Simulated MES + NASA C-MAPSS | Predictive maintenance / OEE |
| [Cascadia Pharmacy](projects/cascadia-pharmacy.qmd) | In Build | CMS Part D + CDC VaxView (real) | Messy-data cleaning / GLP-1 growth |
| [Cascadia Clothing](projects/cascadia-clothing.qmd) | Planned | TBD e-commerce data | Customer segmentation |

---

## Design Principles

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Production patterns only**
Every table, index, and pipeline uses the same conventions you'd enforce on a real production system — surrogate keys, computed `date_key` columns, idempotent DDL, row-count validation scripts.
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Real data where it matters**
CMS Medicare Part D spending and CDC VaxView immunization data are live federal public-domain datasets. Fabricated data is limited to the manufacturing simulation layer (volume, anonymity).
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Repeatable builds**
Each domain ships a `run_phaseN.ps1` script that rebuilds the SQL Server database from scratch — useful for demos, onboarding, and validating migrations.
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**Clean handoff documentation**
Every domain has an `explainer/` doc describing schema decisions, data-cleaning choices, and interview talking points, so a reviewer can follow the reasoning without reading the code.
:::
:::

---

## Tech Stack

<div class="tech-chips">
<span class="chip">SQL Server 2025</span>
<span class="chip">T-SQL</span>
<span class="chip">Microsoft Fabric</span>
<span class="chip">PySpark</span>
<span class="chip">Power BI</span>
<span class="chip">DAX</span>
<span class="chip">Python</span>
<span class="chip">PowerShell</span>
<span class="chip">GitHub Actions</span>
</div>
